content.plain_english: This week in Year 4 maths, your child is working on Addition and subtraction. The learning focuses on: add and subtract numbers with up to four digits using the formal written methods of columnar addition and subtraction where appropriate. These skills build directly on what they have already mastered and prepare them for the next steps in the programme of study.
content.in_school: In lessons, children start with concrete resources — base ten blocks, counters, or number lines — to model calculations physically. They then represent what they have done using drawings and diagrams before practising the formal written method independently. This concrete → pictorial → abstract approach helps the method become secure before moving on.
content.home_activity: Roll two dice twice and use the numbers to make two 2-digit (or 3-digit) numbers. Add them together, then find the difference. Keep a running total over five rounds — who gets the highest? No calculators — the working-out is the fun part.
dinner_table_questions: Can you show me how to add and subtract numbers with up to four digits using the formal written methods of columnar addition and subtraction where appropriate?
What was the trickiest part of addition and subtraction today?
How would you explain today's maths to someone who has never seen it before?

key_vocabulary.definition: The result of adding two or more numbers together.
A written method where numbers are lined up in columns to add.
The result of subtracting one number from another.
A written method where numbers are lined up in columns to subtract.
The opposite operation — addition and subtraction are inverses of each other.

example_questions: Find a common factor of 24 and 36.
content.times_table_tip: Year 4 children should know all their times tables to 12 × 12 by the end of the year — the national Multiplication Tables Check happens in Year 4. Daily 3-minute practice mixing tables in random order is the best preparation.
